text:
coat/lay graphene oxide liquid on any sheet(aluminum sheet, paper, platic, etc) to oven bake 200 degree centigrade (old term for celsius) = 392 degree Fahrenheit.

author: @WaSonFields
text: alright so i am drying another batch right now but i have the picture of the already dried "graphene oxide" if you want to take a look, just don't know how to get you the picture, was also taken from my phone(Galaxy s3).

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: I would be fascinated to see

author: @WaSonFields
text: alright. so after a few days i did two tests.  One with about 5 grams per 50ml, and the second with about 8 grams per 50ml.  After cooking them both for about 2 hours, the solution with 5 grams seemed to look better, or with a finer top layer.  The other one i actually let dry for about  a day in the sun and then i ground up the remains and they look identical.   I will see if i can show you a picture of what it looked like.  Almost looks like carbon tho to be honest.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Cool. Look forward to it

author: @WaSonFields
text: thanks for the quick response! i appreciate all the amazing help you've given.  I will try this when i get a little bit more dried graphene oxide.  I will let you know how the experiment goes.  I ordered the chemicals in order to do a top down approach for graphene oxide as well so i will post when i get any results from that experiment as well.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: I waited ages before buying a sonicator at £30 and then i wondered why i waited. A sonicator is about the only way i have found to properly isperse the grpahite oxide into water to form graphene oxide. Without this dispersion it is impossible - or at least I found it so - to disperse the oxide.

author: @08maxhr

author: @DanielbenDaniel
text: PS it does not matter if the strobe light is amber it just needs to be xenon bulb. I have not tried making plate cells yet because of the difficulties I am having with the graphene impregnated char cloth.

author: @DanielbenDaniel
text: I left a 3 part message on your supercompacitor PT 5 video, a how to I reduced graphene with a bio char cloth & how it worked. also we have been emailing I am under smurphy6969@gmail.  But another way I got some results was painted graphene on Corona plasma treated HDPE film, the treated film lets ink stick to it, Put the 2" squares inside a 120v 40w amber strobe light lens housing. let it strobe for 2 days, it reduced with out melting the HD film. Not sure how good but it darkened alot

author: @DanielbenDaniel
text: Well back to the drawling board.. the plasma reduction was to much for the sub straight. burned traces right through the film. it burned very similar to the intercalated graphite in the micro wave...once a spot reduced it made full contact hot spot, shot lighting sparks, guess i'll have to try a much slower option. darn that sucks! I wanted to spit out 345 graphene plates a min .lol .... oh hey, I just figured a cheap easy way to reduce but it takes a while. you need to try it for us? .email me

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Use the same method to coat fine woven fibreglass in graphene. The set up an alcohol lamp with ethanol as the fuel. Pass the graphene coated fibreglass a few times just above the flame. The soot will form oriented nano tube perpendicular to the cloth. This should be a very good material for batteries

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Graphene is graphite 1 atom thick, as you say. And yes they stick together again. The trick is to get the sheet as thin as possible. Nobody actually wants a sheet of grahene as 1 layer there are just too many problems with it. What is wanted is a sheet 2 or 3 layers thick with the platelets of graphine overlapping or bridged by carbon nanotubes

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: I dripped the GO solution onto the glass and swirled it around until I could see it was coated. Then I put it into the sun to dry then I put it into the oven for 1 hour. Not a good method I know. But it was more a proof of concept. Tomorrow I am going to build a spin coating machine and I will clean the glass with acetone first. I have some iron doped carbon nitride I made I am thinking of using this. Do you want a video on how to make this material?

author: @DavidAlsh
